methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Exodys Energy is a seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Carl Perez and Jean-Christophe Piroux that has raised $10M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 total peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable approach here. Using total raised ($10M) as a proxy for entry valuation and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $25M. The peer-median approach does not apply; while 8 same-sector peers exist, insufficient clustering at identical or adjacent stages and comparable valuation tiers prevents reliable median extraction without risking distortion from outliers in the nuclear sector's wide performance dispersion.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median point of $25M. This range reflects a single methodological input, which introduces meaningful dependence risk. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to the broader nuclear peer set, capital intensity and runway adequacy, and regulatory pathway clarity. A more robust triangulation would benefit from additional comparable data at the seed stage within nuclear, or from revenue/traction metrics if available.
